description: Supply-chain exposure posture from Bumblebee scans (real findings) + simulated exposure built from the perplexityai/bumblebee threat_intel catalogs.

graphs.title: About this dashboard
graphs.markdown:
Two data populations are visualised side by side:

- **Real findings** — emitted by `bumblebee_deep_scan.sh` running under RemoteOps and ingested as `RecordType='BumblebeeFinding'` events.
- **Simulated exposure** — synthetic events derived from the public `perplexityai/bumblebee/threat_intel/*.json` catalogs (654 entries across 8 catalogs). Ingested as `RecordType='BumblebeeSimulatedExposure'` via the bundled `generate_simulated_exposure.py` + `ingest_to_sdl.sh`.

Empty panels on the **Real findings** tab when no agent has run yet are the **expected SOC-positive posture**.

graphs.title: Real findings by catalog
graphs.query: RecordType='BumblebeeFinding' | group n=count() by catalog | sort -n

graphs.title: Simulated exposure by severity
graphs.query: RecordType='BumblebeeSimulatedExposure' | group n=count() by severity | sort -n

graphs.title: What's on this tab
graphs.markdown: Every row is a record emitted by `bumblebee scan --findings-only` and tagged by the wrapper with `RecordType='BumblebeeFinding'`. A panel returning zero is the **desired posture** — non-zero is the regression worth investigating.

graphs.title: Findings by host (split by catalog)
graphs.query: RecordType='BumblebeeFinding' | let catalog_safe = replace_all(catalog, '-', '_') | group n=count() by hostname, catalog_safe | sort hostname | transpose catalog_safe on hostname

graphs.title: Findings by catalog
graphs.query: RecordType='BumblebeeFinding' | group n=count() by catalog | sort -n

graphs.title: Findings detail (latest 200)
graphs.query: RecordType='BumblebeeFinding' | columns timestamp, hostname, catalog, os_id, os_version, message | sort -timestamp | limit 200

graphs.title: What's on this tab
graphs.markdown: All 654 catalog entries from `perplexityai/bumblebee/threat_intel/*.json`, ingested as synthetic events via `ingest_to_sdl.sh`. Each event represents one (catalog, ecosystem, package, version, severity) tuple as if a host had it installed — useful to validate dashboard queries and as a coverage view of *what could be found*, independent of whether any real scan has run yet.

graphs.title: Entries by ecosystem
graphs.query: RecordType='BumblebeeSimulatedExposure' | group n=count() by ecosystem | sort -n

graphs.title: Entries by catalog (top 10)
graphs.query: RecordType='BumblebeeSimulatedExposure' | group n=count() by catalog | sort -n | limit 10

graphs.title: Catalog x severity
graphs.query: RecordType='BumblebeeSimulatedExposure' | group n=count() by catalog, severity | sort catalog | transpose severity on catalog

graphs.title: Top packages by version fanout
graphs.query: RecordType='BumblebeeSimulatedExposure' | group versions=estimate_distinct(version), severities=estimate_distinct(severity) by catalog, ecosystem, package | sort -versions | limit 50

graphs.title: What's on this tab
graphs.markdown: One row per host per scan run, emitted as `RecordType='BumblebeeScanSummary'` by the wrapper script. Use this tab to confirm the scan actually ran on every endpoint you expect.

graphs.title: Latest scan per host
graphs.query: RecordType='BumblebeeScanSummary' | group last=max(end_utc), findings=max(total_findings), inventory=max(inventory_lines), catalogs=max(catalogs_scanned) by hostname, os_id, os_version | sort -last | limit 200

graphs.title: Threat intel catalogs covered
graphs.markdown:
Source: https://github.com/perplexityai/bumblebee/tree/main/threat_intel

| Catalog | Entries | Severity | Ecosystems |
| --- | ---:| --- | --- |
| `antv-mini-shai-hulud` | 324 | all critical | npm (323), pypi (1) |
| `gemstuffer` | 123 | all high | rubygems |
| `laravel-lang-2026-05-23` | 4 | all critical | packagist (huge version fanout) |
| `mini-shai-hulud` | 172 | all critical | npm (170), pypi (2) |
| `node-ipc-credential-stealer` | 1 | critical | npm |
| `nx-console-vscode-2026-05-18` | 1 | critical | editor-extension |
| `shopsprint-decimal-typosquat` | 1 | critical | go |
| `trapdoor-crypto-stealer` | 28 | all critical | npm (21), pypi (7) |

**Totals**: 654 entries / 531 critical + 123 high / 6 ecosystems.

graphs.title: How to refresh
graphs.markdown:
1. Pull the latest catalogs and regenerate the simulated event stream:
   ```bash
   python3 generate_simulated_exposure.py --out simulated_exposure.jsonl
   ```
2. Ingest into SDL via the Log Write key:
   ```bash
   SDL_TOKEN=xxxx ./ingest_to_sdl.sh simulated_exposure.jsonl
   ```
3. Run the real scan via RemoteOps (`bumblebee_deep_scan.sh`).
4. Verify with PowerQuery:
   ```
   RecordType='BumblebeeFinding' or RecordType='BumblebeeSimulatedExposure' or RecordType='BumblebeeScanSummary'
   | group c=count() by RecordType
   | sort -c
   ```
